prompt_replacements.old: Write answer_subject.subject_text as a concise catalog-blind kind of candidate instance established by the current question or conversation resolution, without modifiers that narrow the requested instances.
prompt_replacements.new: Write answer_subject.subject_text as one singular catalog-blind candidate kind, without modifiers that narrow the candidates.

prompt_replacements.old:
answer_population membership_tests define the independently qualifying candidate instances.
answer_population.counted_unit names one business unit in that population.
answer_population.membership_tests must include one SUBJECT_IDENTITY test.
prompt_replacements.new: answer_population.membership_tests contains only explicit user-stated predicates that qualify candidates independently. Candidate identity comes from answer_subject.

prompt_replacements.old:
For NORMAL_BUSINESS_INSTANCE, include a NORMAL_INSTANCE_GUARD test; the backend attaches the standard ORDINARY_BUSINESS_INSTANCE_V1 profile with typed excluded-state roles.
For RAW_DATA_RECORD, include a RAW_RECORD_GUARD test.
prompt_replacements.new: The backend derives the applicable normal-instance or raw-record guard from answer_subject.instance_interpretation.

prompt_replacements.old:
SUBJECT_IDENTITY asks only whether the candidate is an instance of answer_subject and has no input operands.
An input identifying a related entity uses POPULATION_TESTS unless group_key.value_source.question_input_refs owns it as a specified group member.
SUBJECT_IDENTITY, NORMAL_INSTANCE_GUARD, and RAW_RECORD_GUARD use an empty question_input_refs array.
prompt_replacements.new: An input identifying a related entity uses POPULATION_TESTS unless group_key.value_source.question_input_refs owns it as a specified group member.
